summary.intent: Review and act on Codex's automated code review findings for PR #663, specifically investigating and fixing any bugs identified in the focus scope component.
summary.outcome: Identified and fixed a P1 bug where TrapFocus/ReleaseTrap state transitions caused the FocusTrapListener effect to be torn down and never reinstalled, breaking Tab trapping. Fixed with TDD (red→green), updated spec documentation, passed mutation testing, and committed the changes.

repo: The ars-leptos adapter's handle_effects drains all active cleanups when state_changed is true, meaning any state transition that doesn't re-emit an effect will cause that effect's cleanup to run without reinstallation.
Focus scope has a spec document at spec/components/utility/focus-scope.md with sections for transitions (§1.5, §1.6) and an Effect Contract (§1.8) that must be kept in sync with implementation.
The project uses a mutation testing gate (xci-fast) as part of the pre-push workflow, requiring 0 surviving mutants.

code.finding: handle_effects drains all active cleanups on state_changed=true — effects must be re-emitted on every state transition that should keep them active, not just on initial entry.
TrapFocus/ReleaseTrap transitions were only changing state without re-emitting Effect::FocusTrapListener, causing silent trap listener teardown.

workflow: Codex automated review surfaced the P1 bug as an unresolved thread after PR review — monitoring Codex verdict via background task is an effective workflow.
TDD red→green cycle was used: failing tests written first, confirmed red, then fix applied and confirmed green before committing.
Mutation testing (22 killed, 0 surviving, 11 unviable) serves as a post-fix quality gate beyond unit tests.

friction: The adapter's effect cleanup behavior (drain all on state_changed) is a non-obvious footgun — any new state transition implicitly risks silently breaking effects that aren't explicitly re-emitted.
open_items: The underlying adapter design (draining all cleanups on any state change) may warrant a more surgical fix or at least a prominent warning, since the same bug pattern could affect other effects beyond FocusTrapListener.
